annotations.label: Probability of coin landing on edge

anchor.snippet: A computational model suggests that the chance of a coin landing on its edge and staying there is about 1 in 6,000 for an American nickel.

annotations.label: Thickness-to-diameter ratio of fair three-sided coin

anchor.snippet: The ratio of the thickness of the coin to its diameter is thought to be between 0.577 and 0.866.
The three-way flip is 75% likely to work each time it is tried
in flipping by hand people generate a slight bias
their theoretical analysis of the physics of coin tosses predicts a slight bias for a caught coin to be caught the same way up as it was thrown, with a probability of around 0.51

annotations.label: TTH precedes THT twice as often

anchor.snippet: it is twice as likely that the triplet TTH will occur before THT than after it

annotations.label: THH precedes HHT three times as often

anchor.snippet: It is three times as likely that "THH will precede HHT" than that "THH will follow HHT"
The mathematical abstraction of the statistics of coin flipping is described by means of the Bernoulli process
a single flip of a coin is a Bernoulli trial
